channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: International Olympic Committee recommends ending Belarus restrictions
item.description: International Olympic Committee urges sports to let Belarusian athletes compete again without vetting as neutrals.

item.title: US-Israel war on Iran leaves Jordan’s Petra nearly empty of tourists
item.description: Middle East war devastates tourism in Petra, leaving sites empty and Jordanians facing severe loss of income.

item.title: PSG’s Champions League win marred by night of violence and riots in Paris
item.description: Paris Saint-Germain claimed a 6-5 Champions League semifinal win over Bayern Munich, but it erupted in domestic trouble.

item.title: Humanoid robot becomes Buddhist monk in South Korea
item.description: A Buddhist temple in Seoul introduced South Korea’s first humanoid robot monk.

item.title: Chad declares national mourning after deadly Boko Haram ambush
item.description: The killing of two generals near Lake Chad follows a recent Boko Haram attack in the same region that left dozens dead.

item.title: Somali pirates abandon hijacked UAE dhow due to dwindling supplies
item.description: The lemon-laden Fahad-4, hijacked in late April, was used as a mothership to attack other ships.

item.title: Al-Qaeda-linked fighters in Mali set fire to food trucks
item.description: Al-Qaeda-linked fighters have set fire to food trucks as they continue to blockade roads around the capital Bamako, cutt

item.title: Ancient Ziggurat of Ur restored in Iraq using traditional methods
item.description: Restoration work is underway at the ancient Ziggurat of Ur, a monumental stepped temple in Iraq.

item.title: Ivory Coast dissolves electoral body amid criticism
item.description: New agency yet to be named to oversee elections after disbanded commission accused of not being independent.

item.title: Why is the US seeking UN help to open Hormuz after undermining it?
item.description: Marco Rubio has called on the UN for a Hormuz solution as talks for a peace deal in Iran continue.

item.title: All Hail the Military
item.description: A series exploring the systems of power that sustain militarism and the profound impact they have on us all.

item.title: US court releases alleged Jeffrey Epstein ‘suicide note’
item.description: A US court has released what it says is the ‘suicide note’ of Jeffrey Epstein.

item.title: India-administered Kashmir launches campaign to fight youth drug addiction
item.description: India-administered Kashmir launches campaign to fight youth drug addiction

item.title: UK voters head to the polls in local elections
item.description: Voters in England, Scotland and Wales are casting their ballots in local elections.

item.title: MSF accuses Israel of manufacturing a malnutrition crisis in Gaza
item.description: Doctors Without Borders said it identified its first cases of child malnutrition in January 2024, three months into war.

item.title: Irish footballers and celebrities urge boycott of Israel matches
item.description: Irish campaign group accuses Israel of engaging ⁠in 'genocide' in war on Gaza ahead of UEFA Nations League game.

item.title: Turkiye unveils its first intercontinental ballistic missile: What we know
item.description: The Yildirimhan ICBM has a range 6,000km, with a maximum speed of Mach 25 and a payload capacity of 3,000kg.

item.title: Can Asian economies cope with the fallout from the Iran war?
item.description: Fuel costs, inflation and debt pressures are testing Asian economies.

item.title: Africa sees winners and losers as Iran war pushes up oil prices
item.description: Several African countries have sought financial help due to economic uncertainty caused by the war on Iran.

item.title: Passenger from Hantavirus-hit ship speaks to Al Jazeera from isolation
item.description: A passenger from the cruise ship hit with a Hantavirus outbreak has spoken to Al Jazeera from isolation.

item.title: What are US proposals to end war, and will Iran agree to them?
item.description: Iran says it is reviewing a US proposal to end the US-Israel war on it that has caused a global crisis.

item.title: US reinstates deportation proceedings against Mohsen Mahdawi
item.description: Student accuses government of weaponising the immigration system to silence his advocacy for Palestinian rights.

item.title: Madrid captain Carvajal in race against time for Spain’s World Cup squad
item.description: Spain international Dani ⁠Carvajal injured his right foot during a training session for Real Madrid last week.

item.title: What is China’s anti-sanctions law and how does it work?
item.description: Beijing's order blocking US sanctions on five oil refineries marks first invocation of 2021 law.

item.title: Iran’s stance on US war negotiations explained
item.description: Iran’s stance on US war negotiations explained
